title: Woodside Output Hit by Weather Disruptions but LNG Megaprojects Stay on Track
body: Oops, something went wrong Woodside Energy reported a decline in first-quarter production, weighed down by seasonal weather disruptions, even as the company maintained high operational reliability and continued advancing its major LNG and growth projects. The Australian oil and gas producer delivered production of 45.2 million barrels of oil equivalent (MMboe) in the first quarter of 2026, down 8% from the previous quarter, primarily due to cyclone-related impacts across Western Australia. Despite the drop in output, Woodside emphasized strong operational performance, with key assets including Sangomar, Pluto LNG, and the North West Shelf operating at or near 99% reliability. title: Archrock Inc. (AROC) Gains As Market Dips: What You Should Know
body: Oops, something went wrong Archrock Inc. (AROC) closed at $37.99 in the latest trading session, marking a +1.6% move from the prior day. The stock outpaced the S&P 500's daily loss of 0.49%. Elsewhere, the Dow lost 0.05%, while the tech-heavy Nasdaq lost 0.9%. The natural gas compression services business's stock has climbed by 8.41% in the past month, exceeding the Oils-Energy sector's loss of 4.6% and lagging the S&P 500's gain of 12.8%. The investment community will be closely monitoring the performance of Archrock Inc. in its forthcoming earnings report. The company is scheduled to release its earnings on May 5, 2026.
